Grain prices fell sharply at the exchange yesterday as merchants
reported surplus stocks from the eastern provinces. The governor
has issued a proclamation regarding the regulation of trade.

The annual harvest festival will proceed as planned on Saturday next, weather permitting.
All persons wishing to exhibit produce must register at the town hall by Thursday noon.
LOST: One brown leather satchel near the railway station. Reward offered. Enquire within.
YOUR LOCAL PAPER SINCE 1887 — FRIDAY EDITION
Inventor Claims Future of Travel Has Arrived
In a demonstration witnessed by a small but astonished crowd, the
flying machine constructed by the brothers over the past three years
lifted from the ground and remained aloft for a period of some twelve
seconds before returning safely to earth. The craft, which resembles
nothing so much as a large box kite fitted with a small engine, was
piloted by the elder of the two brothers, who lay prone upon the lower
wing during the flight. Spectators reported hearing the engine sputter
and cough before the machine gathered speed along its launching rail.
"It is only a beginning," he said,
"but we believe that one day men
will cross oceans by air." Such
claims seem fanciful to many, but
could not deny what their eyes
had seen. The machine is to be
exhibited at the county fair next
was observed may have been no
more than a prolonged hop. The
arranged at a date to be fixed.

SPECIAL DISPATCH — FIELD EDITION — NOT FOR SALE
The Automobile of the Future — Available Now
Six Cylinders of Pure American Power and Elegance
The new model from the Chevrolet Motor Company represents the finest achievement in
American automotive engineering. With its powerful six-cylinder engine and luxurious
appointments, this vehicle sets a new standard for comfort and performance on the road.
Available in a range of distinguished colours, the new model is priced within reach of
the discerning American family. Visit your nearest dealer for a demonstration today.
the opportunity to test the new
model on the roads outside the
city and reports that it handles
and the seats are most comfortable
it without reservation to all who
seek the finest in motor travel.

Arrived: SS Meridian from Liverpool with general cargo. Departed: Barque Helena bound for Cape Town.
Expected: SS Fortuna from New York, due Thursday. Detained: Schooner Perseverance, customs inquiry.
After a campaign marked by considerable acrimony on both sides, the
incumbent member was returned to his seat by a margin of fewer than
three hundred votes. His opponent has declined to concede, citing
irregularities at several polling stations in the eastern wards. A
formal petition to the electoral court is expected to be filed.
— New railway timetable comes into effect Monday next. See page 3.
— Inquest into mill accident returns verdict of misadventure. Page 5.
— Cricket: County eleven defeats visitors by an innings. Full report p.8.
— New public baths to open in spring. Alderman speaks. Page 2.
— Obituary: Mr. Josiah Pemberton, merchant, aged 74. Page 6.
WANTED: Experienced compositor for busy printing works. Apply in person mornings only.
FOR SALE: Pair of matched bay horses, sound in wind and limb. Enquire at the livery stable.
ROOMS TO LET: Two furnished rooms, respectable house, near station. References required.
NOTICE: The firm of Blackwood & Sons has removed to new premises at 14 High Street.
